title: Live or dead?
pedagogy: Tone rules (1/4): live vs dead syllables. Live = long open vowel or sonorant final (n, m, ng, y, w), plus the special vowels ไ◌ ใ◌ เ◌า ◌ำ; dead = short vowel or stop final (p, t, k). Prerequisite classification for every tone rule.

exercises.title: Syllables are live or dead
body: Before tones, Thai asks one question of every syllable: does it ring on, or stop short?
LIVE syllables end open on a long vowel (กา) or in a humming sonorant final — n, m, ng, y, w (กิน, ลาน).
DEAD syllables end abruptly: a short vowel (กะ) or a stop final p, t, k (นก, จาก).

exercises.title: Why it matters
body: Live and dead syllables take different tones — even when no tone mark is written.
The full table comes in the next lesson. First, make the live/dead call itself a reflex.
Rule of thumb: long-or-sonorant = live; short-or-stop = dead. And remember the four special vowels ไ◌ ใ◌ เ◌า ◌ำ — written short, but they count as live.

exercises.promptNote: ka — long า, no final consonant
exercises.question: Live or dead?
exercises.explanation: กา ends open on the long vowel า, so it rings on — live.

exercises.question: Live or dead?
exercises.explanation: The short vowel ะ cuts the syllable off — dead.

exercises.question: Live or dead?
exercises.explanation: กิน ends in the sonorant น (n), so it is live even though ◌ิ is short.

exercises.question: Live or dead?
exercises.explanation: นก (read with an unwritten short o) ends in the stop ก (k) — dead. Stop finals p, t, k always kill the ring.

exercises.question: Live or dead?
exercises.explanation: Long า with an open end — live.

exercises.question: Live or dead?
exercises.explanation: Despite the long า, the stop final ก makes จาก dead.

exercises.question: Live or dead?
exercises.explanation: ◌ำ ends in an m sound — one of the four written-short vowels that count as live.

exercises.question: Live or dead?
exercises.explanation: ไ◌ counts as live — it ends in a y-glide.

exercises.question: Live or dead?
exercises.explanation: Short ะ with no final — dead.

exercises.question: Live or dead?
exercises.explanation: The sonorant final น keeps it ringing — live.
